EggScore
Owner & Staff Access
Accounts are created by EggScore directly — there is no self-signup.
Raise Commission Dispute
⚠ Raising a dispute flags this rep's commission for review and holds payout pending resolution. All actions are logged in the Event Log.
Resolve Commission Dispute
Deboard Sales Rep
⚠ Deboarding removes the rep from all active dropdowns. Their historical sales, commission records, and ledger entries are fully preserved in archive.
Settle Credit Transaction
⚠ Partial payment — balance will remain outstanding in credit register.
✓ Full settlement — transaction will be closed and marked paid.
Reverse Settlement
Cancel Order
⚠ This closes the order without it becoming a sale and releases its crates back to available stock immediately. The order record is kept — nothing is deleted.

—
No government ID is collected. This CID follows the same BEL-XXX-YYMMDD-HHMMSS convention already used for every order, invoice, and waybill — a unique internal reference, generated automatically, nothing to ask the customer for.

Driver Input
Authorization — Digital Sign-Off
Draw with mouse or finger. All three needed to complete the log.
🏷️ Carton Branding Readiness
Unbranded eggs sit in Nigeria's VAT-exempt basic-foodstuff category. The moment a carton carries the EggScore name, that exemption is gone — permanently, on every sale, not just the branded batch. Staying unbranded is the deliberate choice for now; this just tracks the real trailing volume against where that trade-off is expected to flip, so the decision gets revisited on real data, not forgotten.
Buyer Tier Contract
🧮 VAT Summary Worksheet
💼 Commission / Payout Register
🏦 Bank Reconciliation Worksheet
🌾 Generate Pickup Waybill
Pickup Waybill / Goods Receipt
Write Off Expired Stock
💀 This permanently removes the recorded quantity from inventory and the batch register. This action cannot be undone — it represents physical stock that has left the warehouse and can no longer be sold.
Defaults to full remaining balance — edit if only partial spoilage occurred.
Estimated cost value lost: ₦0
🔑 Owner Override — Credit Suspension
🚫 This customer's credit is suspended. A rep cannot proceed. This override is recorded permanently in the audit log with your name, the reason, and the timestamp.
🔑 Owner Verification Required
🚫 This order matches one or more risk signals from the reservation abuse checks. A rep cannot proceed without owner verification, recorded permanently with your name, the reason, and the timestamp.
🛑 Stop-Loss — Owner Authorisation Required
🚫 This sale draws from a batch blend below the margin floor, and the real-currency exposure exceeds your configured ceiling. It cannot save without owner sign-off, recorded permanently with the reason, amount, and timestamp.
💳 Verify Payment
⚠ Only verify after confirming the credit has appeared in the actual BMF EggScore Limited bank account — not from a screenshot alert alone.
⏳ Log Pending Transfer
Customer claims payment sent but credit not yet showing in our account. Log the transaction reference and place the order on hold. Only the designated authority can release goods before the credit posts.
Found on the customer's bank receipt or debit alert — this is what distinguishes a genuine pending transfer from a fake alert.
📄 Import Bank Statement
Upload a CSV export of your bank statement. BMF EggScore Limited scans the narration field on each credit for an order reference (BEL-ORD-…) and proposes matches below — nothing is verified until you review and confirm.
Export from your bank's app or internet banking → Statement → Download CSV. Column layout is auto-detected — GTBank, Access and Zenith exports all work.
0 rows
🔑 Release Held Order
This releases the order for fulfilment before payment has been confirmed in the bank account. Owner PIN required. Action is permanently logged.
💰 Record Overpayment
The excess above the invoice amount will be credited to the customer's wallet and applied automatically against their next order. No cash leaves the business — this is a credit balance, not a refund.
🔑 Owner Authorisation
PIN locked after 3 failed attempts for 30 minutes. A security alert will be sent via WhatsApp.
🔑 Change Owner PIN
A new recovery code will be generated when you change your PIN. Write it down and store it securely — it is shown only once.
Your Recovery Code — Write This Down Now
This code will NOT be shown again. Store it somewhere safe, separate from this device. It can only be used once to reset your PIN if you forget it.
🔓 Reset PIN via Recovery Code
Recovery codes are single-use. Once used, the old code is immediately invalidated and a new one is generated with the new PIN.
🔑 Edit Payment Terms
Changing a customer's payment terms requires owner authorisation. This change is permanently logged — who changed it, when, and what it changed from.
📱 Edit Contact Info
Close — No Sale
This records that the request was followed up but did not result in a sale — for example, the customer sourced elsewhere, pricing didn't work, or timing no longer fits. It will not create a sale or affect inventory.

Price History
Batch Margin Table at current selling price
Minimum Margin Floor
Alert fires when any batch margin falls below this threshold
₦/crate
Target Margin Ceiling
Margin at low order volume — drives the suggested wholesale price and the top of the volume discount curve
⚠ Ceiling must be above the floor — not saved
₦/crate
📈 Volume Discount Pricing
Margin decays from the ceiling toward the floor as order size grows. Every portal order is priced live off this curve.
🎯 Sweet Spot — Diminishing Returns
The curve is concave the whole way — no true inflection point — but by 3–5× the half-life, nearly all the available discount is already captured. Past that, a bigger order barely moves the price. Useful as a reference point for promo minimums, solo-dispatch thresholds, or just knowing where a customer's "just one more crate" logic stops paying off.
🛑 Stop-Loss Exposure Ceiling
A sale drawing from a floor-breaching batch blend is blocked and needs owner PIN sign-off once total exposure (crates × shortfall/crate) crosses this ₦ ceiling — a hard gate, not just a banner.
₦ total
📡 Price-Watch Deviation Threshold
The Command Centre's "Price vs. Suggested" tile turns red once your live selling price drifts from the current suggestion by more than this — e.g. after a batch rolls over via FIFO and the suggestion moves but the price doesn't.
₦/crate
🐢 Price Velocity Cap — Daily Ramp Step
When a batch rollover pushes the suggested price up, "Start Gradual Ramp" lets it walk to the new level instead of jumping in one step. This is how much of the remaining gap closes per day — higher means faster catch-up, lower means smoother for customers but more margin absorbed along the way. Increases only; a cheaper batch still drops the price instantly.
% / day

🚚 Distribution Pricing Model
one flat rate for pooled routes · solo dispatches price off real cost
Zone A/B/C is retired as a customer-facing rate table. Customers now see one flat delivery fee — landing cost is what they compare, not the logistics behind it. The floor and ceiling below are what a rep can quote on a pooled, full-van route; anything at or above the solo-dispatch threshold gets its own real-cost check instead.
📊 Delivery Volume Analysis
actual crates/order, collect vs deliver — checks pricing assumptions against reality

FIFO Batch Register

Inventory Valuation Report
FIFO vs Weighted Average Cost · Kobo precision
FIFO Valuation
—
batch-specific cost
WAC Valuation
—
blended average cost
Variance
—
FIFO − WAC
BatchSupplierQty Remaining Unit Cost (FIFO)FIFO Value WAC Unit CostWAC ValueStatus
METHODOLOGY:
FIFO valuation values each batch at its actual recorded purchase cost — this is the figure that should be used for balance sheet reporting and tax purposes, as it reflects the true historical cost of goods on hand.
WAC valuation values all remaining stock at the blended average cost across all active batches — useful for pricing decisions and understanding overall margin exposure, but not the figure for formal accounts.
Variance shows the difference between the two methods. A positive variance means FIFO values inventory higher than WAC would (older batches were more expensive); negative means newer/active batches are pricier than the blend.

⚙ Tier Thresholds
starter defaults — tune as real data accumulates, nothing here is fixed
Biosecurity thresholds only apply once a supplier's checklist has actually been filled in via the Suppliers screen — an unassessed supplier isn't penalized for a checklist nobody's completed yet.
📞 Multi-Supplier Comparison Reminder
largest volume → lowest quote, per the rearrangement math
For a weighted blend, pairing your biggest volume with your cheapest quote genuinely minimizes blended cost — not just a preference, a real mathematical result. This can't verify you actually called around; it's a checkpoint that stops you before committing a large order without having deliberately made that comparison.
crates triggers the reminder

Promotions Engine

Targeted deals · segment-based offers · customer incentives
Active Promotions
0 live
Promotion History
all campaigns
Promo ROI Tracking
cost vs uplift · sales velocity
METHODOLOGY:
Promo Cost = discount/crate × crates sold under this promo.
Promo Revenue = post-discount egg revenue from promo-tagged sales.
Sales Velocity compares average crates/day in the period immediately before the promo started vs during the promo's active window — a directional signal of whether overall sales activity increased.
Net Impact = Promo Revenue − Promo Cost. This is a conservative, rule-based estimate — not a controlled experiment — and should be read alongside the velocity trend rather than in isolation.

P&L Calculator

Revenue minus real cost elements — pulled from actual Sales Log data where it already exists, built to grow as more cost lines become known
Period
every figure below recalculates for whatever range is set here
Revenue
pulled live from Sales Log — nothing entered manually
Cost of Goods Sold
real batch cost of whatever was actually sold in period
Fixed Assets — Depreciation
straight-line, prorated to the period above
Other Cost Elements
statutory, regulatory, operational — name and value them as they become known
Add a row for anything not covered above — licensing, levies, insurance, rent, whatever surfaces. Leave the label blank until you know what it actually is; a blank/zero row is simply excluded from the total below, not treated as an error.
Result
Revenue − COGS − Depreciation − Other Costs

ℹ Every document here generates as a real, downloadable PDF — nothing opens a print dialog you have to configure. Documents marked "instant" need no form; the rest ask for the minimum needed (a customer, a sale, a date range) before generating.
